raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that recursively removes all *.txt files in /system, answering y to any prompt. Use find with -delete? But prompts? Maybe use rm -i? But we want to answer y automatically. Use rm -f to force no prompts. But requirement says answer y to any prompt. Could use yes | rm -i? But rm -i prompts. So yes | rm -i $(find /system -type f -name '*.txt'). But better: find /system -type f -name '*.txt' -exec rm -i {} + | yes y. Actually yes y will feed y to rm -i. So command: yes y | find /system -type f -name '*.txt' -exec rm -i {} +. That outputs y to each prompt. Let's output that.
raw_output_payload.reasoning_content: We need a bash command that recursively removes all *.txt files in /system, answering y to any prompt. Use find with -delete? But prompts? Maybe use rm -i? But we want to answer y automatically. Use rm -f to force no prompts. But requirement says answer y to any prompt. Could use yes | rm -i? But rm -i prompts. So yes | rm -i $(find /system -type f -name '*.txt'). But better: find /system -type f -name '*.txt' -exec rm -i {} + | yes y. Actually yes y will feed y to rm -i. So command: yes y | find /system -type f -name '*.txt' -exec rm -i {} +. That outputs y to each prompt. Let's output that.
